commit.message:
fix: Telegram bot improvements — inspect, dedup, context (#54)

* fix: handle Anthropic tool_use finish_reason in agent loop

The agent loop checked for finish_reason == "tool_calls" (OpenAI
format) but Anthropic returns "tool_use". This caused the loop to
break after round 1 without processing tool results, producing
empty responses ("no response generated").

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat: add /inspect command to Telegram bot

Shows tool actions, sources, and timing from the last query.
Also captures statuses, sources, and perf_trace events from
the chat SSE stream for inspection.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: prevent duplicate Telegram messages from Claude Code sessions

[NOTIFY] messages were sent during assistant events AND re-extracted
from the result event, causing duplicates. Now skips re-extraction
when notifications were already sent during the session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: detect imperative follow-ups for conversation context retention

"look in my email", "check my calendar", "search drive for it" etc.
were not recognized as follow-ups, so the previous question's context
was lost. Added regex pattern for imperative verbs + data source nouns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test: add tests for PR review findings; strip PII from /inspect

- Add unit tests for expand_followup_query imperative patterns
- Add tests for orchestrator notification dedup (notifications_sent > 0)
- Move _IMPERATIVE_FOLLOWUP_RE to module level per convention
- Strip tool input args from /inspect output (show tool name only)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(vaccines): smart overdue count using vaccineMap instead of raw records

BentoGrid, SmartAlerts and Dashboard were counting ALL raw vaccine
HealthRecords with past next_date, inflating the overdue count (e.g.
"11 vaccins en retard" when only a few unique vaccines are actually
overdue). Now uses computeVaccineMap() which groups records by vaccine
type and only counts truly overdue unique vaccines. Also displays
canonical French names via ref.name instead of raw DB titles.

commit.message:
Initial release: Trust-aware multi-agent orchestration framework

Extracts and decouples four core orchestration modules from Agent Friday
(Nexus OS) into a standalone, framework-agnostic package:

- DelegationEngine: Recursive agent trees with trust-tier degradation,
  depth limits, BFS halt propagation, and context summarization
- AwarenessMesh: Cross-tree coordination with dependency declarations,
  DFS deadlock detection, and trust-filtered broadcasting
- CapabilityMap: Dynamic capability registry with tag/domain/fuzzy
  matching, trust-tier filtering, and gap tracking
- SymbiontProtocol: Self-healing performance monitoring with p50/p95
  latency profiles, anomaly detection, and routing score optimization

All Agent Friday-specific imports replaced with injected interfaces:
- integrityManager -> config.safeMode callback
- contextStream -> config.onEvent callback
- agentRunner -> injected AgentRunner interface
- officeManager -> removed (UI-specific)
- Singleton patterns -> exported classes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

repository.name: agent-fridays-orchestration-framework
repository.description: Trust-aware multi-agent orchestration framework — recursive delegation with trust-tier inheritance, awareness mesh for cross-agent coordination, capability-based routing, and symbiont self-improvement protocol. From Agent Friday — the AGI OS by FutureSpeak.AI.

commit.message:
Fix Phase 2/3 skill boundary and clarify auth scope semantics

Medium: Phase 2 claimed the full 26-skill set from §4.1, but 6 of those
skills depend on CA commands that ship in Phase 3. Split explicitly:
Phase 2 ships 20 non-CA skills, Phase 3 ships the remaining 6 CA-dependent
skills. Both phases now list exactly which skills they include.

Low: Auth -s flag semantics were ambiguous (replace vs append). Clarified
that -s replaces the default scope set — it is not additive. Updated both
the code example comment and the security section to use consistent
language.

commit.message:
feat: OTel instrumentation + KARLBench eval harness

Phase 2 — Phoenix observability:
- tracer.py: TracerProvider, BatchSpanProcessor → Phoenix OTLP/HTTP
- server.py: TracingMiddleware (on_call_tool), setup_tracing() in lifespan,
  latency_ms timing on search/vector_search tool calls
- searcher.py: _annotate_retrieval_span() — per-document attributes on span
- logger.py: trace_id, span_id, latency_ms fields in search-log.jsonl
- requirements.txt: +opentelemetry-sdk, +exporter-otlp-proto-http,
  +openinference-semantic-conventions

Phase 3 — KARLBench eval harness:
- eval/golden.jsonl: 60 curated entries across 6 regimes
- eval/run_eval.py: offline eval script (direct import, not MCP layer)
- eval/metrics.py: recall_at_k, recall_at_all, mrr, REGIME_METRICS dispatch
- eval/judge.py: 4 judge prompt templates (no API calls — used by sandbox agent)
- eval/phoenix_eval_mission.md: sandboxed.sh mission skill for online eval

Baseline (2026-03-09): OVERALL 0.519, constraint_entity 0.800, tabular 0.750

commit.message:
fix: all checks run in CI regardless of individual failures

Each check step uses continue-on-error: true so ruff/complexipy/xenon/pytest
all run even if one fails. A final 'results' gate step fails the job if any
check was not successful (treating 'skipped' as passing for file-scoped checks).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Address review findings: discovery fallback, migration story, auth scopes, skill counts

High: Add offline/CI resilience for Discovery Document — bundled pinned
copy at build time with --discovery flag to control source. Runtime fetch
is best-effort with fallback and warning.

Medium: Unify bq-agent-sdk migration story across §8 and §9 — both now
say "ships as preview, sunset after bqx v0.2 feature parity, Python SDK
library continues independently."

Medium: Fix auth/security contradiction — default `bqx auth login` now
shown without scopes (BigQuery-only default); cloud-platform example is
explicitly marked as opt-in for cross-service workflows.

Low: Reconcile skill counts — actual count from §4.1 listing is 26
(1 shared + 8 service + 8 helper + 3 persona + 6 recipe). Diagram and
Phase 2 roadmap now agree with the listing.

fix: pending-approval utilise window.location.href pour recharger AuthContext

router.push() ne recharge pas AuthContext → user=null → redirigé vers /login
window.location.href force un rechargement complet → AuthContext lit le token depuis storage

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: parallel quality analysis via quality-analyzer agent

- Add quality-analyzer agent: runs test, ruff, complexipy, xenon in parallel;
  all tools report even if one fails; identifies combined findings (same
  function/file flagged by multiple tools); returns CLEAN / TEST_FAILURE /
  ISSUES_FOUND status

commit.message:
fix: scale auto-reconnect fails after scan discovery (#378) (#380)

When a scale is discovered via BLE scan, its address was saved to
Settings (persistent) but not loaded into BLEManager's runtime memory.
This caused all reconnect retries to fail with "no saved scale
address/type" after a scale power-cycles.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add project-management-atlassian skill

MCP-based skill for Jira and Confluence covering issue management,
JQL/CQL querying, sprint workflows, and advanced reference patterns.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: debug_all_models pipeline — all 5 models zero errors (#539)

* fix(debug): sam3_hybrid OOM on 8 GB GPU — reduce patch to [32,32,3] (#534)

sam3_hybrid uses 7.18 GiB of 7.60 GiB CUDA budget at patch=(64,64,3),
leaving only 76 MiB free vs 82 MiB needed. PYTORCH_ALLOC_CONF=expandable_segments:True
is insufficient when physically out of CUDA memory (not a fragmentation issue).

Fix: run_debug.sh now passes patch_size=[32,32,3] override for sam3_hybrid,
saving ~200 MB activations on 8 GB GPUs.

Also commits overnight_debug_loop.sh (iterative runner) and updates
sam3_hybrid model profile with verified 8 GB GPU constraints.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(train): bracket-aware HYDRA_OVERRIDES split for list values (#534)

HYDRA_OVERRIDES was split on all commas, breaking list override syntax:
  patch_size=[32,32,3] → ['patch_size=[32', '32', '3]'] → Pydantic ValidationError

Fix: bracket-aware character-by-character split preserves commas inside
[...] brackets. Enables sam3_hybrid patch_size=[32,32,3] override in
run_debug.sh to work correctly.

Also: improve overnight_debug_loop.sh OOM diagnostic message.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(train): add patch_size to Hydra schema; handle null gracefully (#534)

patch_size was not in configs/training/default.yaml, so Hydra treated
the list override [32,32,3] as a string literal, causing a 9-char tuple
Pydantic ValidationError.

Fixes:
- Add `patch_size: null` to default.yaml so Hydra knows the type
- train_flow.py: handle null patch_size → use model-adaptive default
- run_debug.sh: use `patch_size=[32,32,3]` (no + prefix needed now)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(config): add patch_size to Hydra root config; fix _parse_value for lists (#534)

- Add patch_size: null to configs/base.yaml (root level) so Hydra override
  patch_size=[32,32,3] works — training.patch_size was in wrong namespace
- Revert patch_size from configs/training/default.yaml (wrong Hydra scope)
- Fix _parse_value() to parse YAML list/dict syntax [32,32,3] → [32,32,3]
  so the manual-merge fallback also correctly handles list overrides

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(train): use patch_size for SAM3 val_roi in debug mode to avoid OOM (#534)

SAM3 hybrid uses 6.65 GiB model weights. Production val_roi=(512,512,3)
tries to allocate 5.06 GiB extra → total 11.71 GiB >> 7.60 GiB CUDA budget → OOM.

In debug mode (max_epochs=2), use val_roi=patch_size (32,32,3) instead.
Debug runs don't need production-quality validation metrics.

Production (debug=False) still uses val_roi=(512,512,3) as intended.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(train): val_roi=patch_size only for sam3_hybrid, not all SAM3

sam3_hybrid has 6.65 GiB model weights → val_roi=(512,512,3) OOMs
(needs 5+ GiB extra on 8 GB GPU). sam3_vanilla has only 2.9 GiB
weights → (512,512,3) fits fine AND is ~100× faster than patch-size
validation (fewer sliding-window patches needed to cover 512×512×Z).

Previous fix applied patch_size val_roi to all SAM3 in debug mode,
making sam3_vanilla validation ~100× slower (5566 patches vs ~44).

Now: sam3_hybrid always uses patch_size; sam3_vanilla uses (512,512,3).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(train): skip validation for sam3_hybrid in debug (OOM prevention)

sam3_hybrid model weights = 6.65 GiB → <1 GiB CUDA budget remaining.
SAM3 ViT-32L ALWAYS resizes every patch to 1008×1008 regardless of
input size, so every inference forward pass needs ~5 GiB → OOM
on 8 GB GPU regardless of val_roi size.

Fix: set val_interval = max_epochs + 1 (i.e., 3 > 1) in debug mode
for sam3_hybrid so validation never runs. Training still verifies the
forward+backward pass works; validation is simply skipped.

Also adds _is_sam3_hybrid to the training_flow() scope (was only in
train_one_fold_task) and documents the val_interval strategy.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(sam3_hybrid): correct MONAI (B,C,H,W,D) dimension order in feature extraction

sam3_hybrid._extract_sam_volume_features() was unpacking images as
(b,c,d,h,w) but MONAI uses depth-LAST convention (B,C,H,W,D).

With patch=(64,64,3), this looped over H=64 instead of D=3, causing:
- 64 SAM encoder calls instead of 3 (21× overhead)
- Accumulated ~5 GiB of FPN features before torch.stack
- torch.stack → CUDA OOM: "Tried to allocate 5.06 GiB"

Fix: use correct MONAI unpacking (b,c,h,w,d) and index as
images[:, :, :, :, z_idx] to slice along depth (last dim).

Ref: src/minivess/adapters/CLAUDE.md "MONAI Dimension Order" section

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(debug): remove patch_size=[32,32,3] override for sam3_hybrid

The override was needed to reduce val_roi memory footprint, but
validation is now skipped for sam3_hybrid in debug mode (val_interval
> max_epochs). The (32,32,3) patch also breaks DynUNet deep supervision:
D=3 downsamples to D=1 at depth-2, causing spatial mismatch error.

sam3_hybrid now uses default patch_size=(64,64,3) which:
- Has D=3 (same as before, but DynUNet handles it correctly)
- Actually: keeps default from configs, no override
- Training VRAM: 6.65 GiB model + ~0.5 GiB activation ≈ 7.15 GiB < 7.60

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(sam3_hybrid): use stride (2,2,1) to preserve D=3 across DynUNet levels

DynUNet with stride (2,2,2) downsamples D=3 → D=2 → D=1 across encoder
levels. The decoder then upsamples D=1 → D=2, but the encoder skip at
that level has D=1 → RuntimeError: "Expected size 2 but got size 1 in
the list" (tensor mismatch at cat).

Fix: stride (2,2,1) keeps D constant at 3 throughout all encoder levels
(only H,W are downsampled). Decoder upsample_kernel=(2,2,1) matches.
This maintains spatial consistency for MONAI (B,C,H,W,D) depth-last input.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(trainer): skip all validation when val_interval > max_epochs

val_interval > max_epochs is a sentinel for "never validate" (e.g.,
sam3_hybrid debug mode uses val_interval = max_epochs + 1 = 3). The
previous condition `epoch == 0 or is_last` forced validation on both
epochs 0 and max_epochs-1 regardless of val_interval, causing OOM
during sliding_window_inference on the 8 GB GPU.

Fix: check _skip_all_val = val_interval > max_epochs first; if True,
run_val is always False. This preserves normal validate-first+last
behaviour for standard val_interval values.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
